Gaia BH1.notes: Nearest known black hole to Sol, discovered 2022 via Gaia DR3 astrometry. Orbited by Gaia BH1 A, a Sun-like G dwarf, every 186 days. Its wide orbit challenges standard binary evolution models.

Gaia BH3.notes: Most massive stellar-mass black hole found in the Milky Way, discovered 2024. Its extreme mass (~33 M☉) is linked to its metal-poor giant companion LS II +14 13 ([Fe/H] = -2.56). Member of the ED-2 stellar stream.

Gaia BH2.notes: Second nearest known black hole, discovered 2023. Orbited by Gaia BH2 A, a K-giant, every 1,277 days in the widest stellar-mass BH binary known. Like BH1, its wide orbit challenges binary evolution models.
